thought_beat_text: He stands at the wall's edge where the set simply stops, and the darkness beyond it isn't threatening so much as honest — no audience, no geometry, just the hum of equipment that doesn't care what he does next. The pull toward it isn't escape exactly, more like the relief of a word you've been reaching for finally arriving, even if the word turns out to mean nothing. He knows he can't stay at the threshold; the ring is still back there, still waiting with its rehearsed demands, but for one more second he lets the backstage dark be enough.
residue_summary: The backstage dark offered a kind of honesty he wasn't ready to refuse, and that refusal to refuse will follow him back toward the ring.
image_hint: figure silhouetted at the edge of a set wall, facing unlit space, one foot not quite lifted
audio_hint: low equipment hum, no reverb, as if the room has forgotten to perform
